lessons.title: Holding Your Hook & Yarn
lessons.description: Learn a comfortable pencil or knife grip and how to tension yarn.
lessons.content: Practice holding the hook like a pencil or knife. Wrap yarn over your non-dominant index finger to control tension.

lessons.title: Making a Slip Knot
lessons.description: Start every project with a secure slip knot on your hook.
lessons.content: Create a loop, pull yarn through, tighten onto the hook, and leave a short tail.

lessons.title: Chain Stitch (ch)
lessons.description: Foundation chains are the starting row for most flat crochet.
lessons.content: Yarn over, pull through the loop on your hook. Repeat for the number of chains required.

lessons.title: Single Crochet (sc)
lessons.description: The most common stitch for amigurumi and tight fabric.
lessons.content: Insert hook, yarn over, pull up a loop, yarn over, pull through both loops on the hook.

lessons.title: Double Crochet (dc)
lessons.description: Taller stitch used in blankets, garments, and lace.
lessons.content: Yarn over, insert hook, yarn over and pull up a loop, yarn over and pull through two loops twice.

lessons.title: Increasing (inc)
lessons.description: Add stitches in one place to widen your fabric or plushie.
lessons.content: Work two stitches into the same stitch or space to increase stitch count by one.

lessons.title: Decreasing (dec)
lessons.description: Reduce stitch count to taper shapes and close rounds.
lessons.content: Insert hook in next stitch, pull up a loop, repeat in following stitch, yarn over, pull through all loops.

lessons.title: Magic Ring
lessons.description: Start amigurumi with a tight, adjustable center.
lessons.content: Wrap yarn into a ring, crochet into the ring, pull tail to close, mark the first stitch.

lessons.title: Working in Rounds
lessons.description: Use stitch markers and spiral rounds for seamless plushies.
lessons.content: Place a marker in the first stitch of each round and move it up as you work.

lessons.title: Reading Pattern Abbreviations
lessons.description: Decode common crochet shorthand used in patterns and charts.
lessons.content: Learn sc, hdc, dc, tr, inc, dec, MR, and repeat notation like *...* x6.

lessons.title: Weaving In Ends
lessons.description: Secure yarn tails for durable finished projects.
lessons.content: Thread the tail on a needle and weave through several stitches in multiple directions before trimming.

lessons.title: Blocking Basics
lessons.description: Shape and relax fibers for a polished finish.
lessons.content: Pin project to measurements, mist with water or steam lightly, and let dry completely.

projects.title: Dachshund Plushie
projects.description: Amigurumi dachshund with elongated body and floppy ears.

projects.title: Sunflower Bag
projects.description: Market bag with sunflower motif panel and sturdy handles.

projects.title: Granny Blanket
projects.description: Classic granny square blanket in teal, coral, and cream.

projects.title: Cozy Sweater
projects.description: Top-down raglan sweater in soft merino blend.
